risk: local LLM may outperform R4 on strong_action screening
reviewer_question: Why not test Qwen/Llama locally? They might catch more strong_action cases than R4.
current_answer: Possible but not guaranteed. API-based LLMs (GPT-5.5, DeepSeek-V3) already show strong conservatism (strong_recall=0.04 vs R4=0.36). No prior evidence that local LLMs behave differently on this task.
mitigation: List as future work with pre-registered 120-pair pilot protocol; if local LLM strong_recall > 0.20, it becomes a viable complement, not a replacement.

risk: R4 may be too heuristic compared to learned ranker
reviewer_question: R4 uses hand-tuned thresholds and SVM; why not use a learned ranking model?
current_answer: R4 is a screening router, not a precision classifier. Its heuristic nature is transparent and frozen (t_contra=0.48, t_strong=0.535, t_svm=0.51). Learned ranker may improve precision@20 but cannot fix R4 screening failures (32 true strong_action not flagged).
mitigation: Positioning R4 as high-recall first-stage filter; learned ranker listed as future improvement for review queue ordering.

risk: no local LLM baseline in paper
reviewer_question: The paper only has API-based LLM baseline; why no local LLM?
current_answer: API-based LLM baseline (GPT-5.5, DeepSeek-V3) already establishes LLM conservatism finding. Local LLM deployment requires hardware, version freeze, security approval, and parse stability. Current focus is retrieval-to-screening interface, not LLM comparison.
mitigation: Explicitly state in Limitations that local LLM is future work; pre-register pilot protocol in Appendix to show preparedness.

risk: confidential deployment may still use approved LLM
reviewer_question: If deployed in a confidential setting, would R4 still be used without LLM?
current_answer: In confidential deployment, an approved local LLM may replace API-based LLM. R4's value as a screening complement remains: it catches strong_action cases that LLMs miss (8/100 in matched sample), regardless of LLM deployment mode.
mitigation: Describe R4 as deployment-mode-agnostic screening complement; note that local LLM can substitute API-based LLM in confidential settings.

reviewer_question: R4 cannot be used standalone (FP/TP@1%=58.73); is it practical?
current_answer: R4 is explicitly positioned as second-stage screener (FP/TP@5%=11.24, model_recall=0.7064). Standalone use requires upstream filter. This is a design choice, not a defect: high-recall screening + human/LLM second-stage review is the standard pipeline pattern.
mitigation: State second-stage positioning clearly; provide low-prevalence simulation showing viability at 5% prevalence with upstream filter.

reviewer_question: All results are silver; how do we know R4's strong_action signal is real?
current_answer: Silver labels are AI-preannotated development labels. §VII 50-pair two-layer gold pilot is pre-registered (protocol frozen, annotation NOT begun). Gold adjudication will determine Level-2 boundary reliability (κ threshold 0.40). §VII.K fallback rules provide graceful-degradation path.
mitigation: Pre-register gold protocol in §VII; state silver-stage caveat in every result table; commit to gold adjudication as next action.
